title: A City That Never Sleeps
content: Living in the city had taught Ethan that true silence barely existed anymore. Even at three in the morning, distant sirens, traffic, and glowing apartment windows reminded him that thousands of lives continued moving around him constantly. At first, the endless energy of the city had excited him. Over time, however, he began craving moments of stillness that seemed increasingly difficult to find. One night, unable to sleep, Ethan walked through downtown streets filled with restaurants, taxis, and tired workers finishing late shifts. Watching strangers move beneath bright lights, he realized the city itself almost felt alive — restless, impatient, and incapable of ever fully stopping to breathe.
